teachingPoint: Gig acceptance must be BOLA-gated then SETNX-locked then OCC-confirmed. BOLA at ORDER 1 ensures the accepting client owns this gig posting — preventing cross-tenant gig hijacking. SETNX at ORDER 2 ensures only one bid is accepted per gig — preventing concurrent double-acceptance race. OCC at ORDER 3 validates the bid status has not changed since the lock was acquired — preventing a stale bid from being accepted after the freelancer withdrew.
positiveExample: T613: validateBOLA(gigPosting.clientTenantId === ALS.tenantId) at ORDER 1 → SETNX('gig-accept-lock:{gigId}') at ORDER 2 → OCC bid status check at ORDER 3 → storeDocument(audit) at ORDER 4 → enqueue(GigAccepted) at ORDER 5.
negativeExample: T613 skips BOLA check — any authenticated tenant can accept gigs belonging to other tenants. Or T613 skips SETNX — two concurrent accept requests both pass the bid status check and double-accept. Or T613 accepts after bid OCC check fails — stale acceptance of a withdrawn bid.
discriminatingConstraint: BOLA at ORDER 1 before SETNX, SETNX before OCC, OCC before storeDocument. Any reorder = BUILD_FAILURE. Extends CART-LOCK-SETNX-001 from FLOW-16.

teachingPoint: Milestone contracts have CONTRACT_IMMUTABLE_FIELDS that must be validated before any write. Field immutability is a compile-time constant — never a database lookup. Sum validation ensures milestones sum equals contract total — preventing partial-payment escrow gaps. OCC write ensures concurrent milestone edits do not silently overwrite each other.
positiveExample: T614: CONTRACT_IMMUTABLE_FIELDS guard at ORDER 1 → sum validation (milestones sum === contractTotal) at ORDER 2 → OCC write at ORDER 3 → storeDocument(audit) at ORDER 4 → enqueue(FreelancerContractActivated) at ORDER 5.
negativeExample: T614 allows clientId or contractTotal to be overwritten after activation — attacker changes the contract amount mid-engagement. Or T614 skips sum validation — milestones sum to 80% of contract total, 20% escrow gap silently goes unfunded. Or T614 uses plain storeDocument without OCC — concurrent edits silently overwrite each other.
discriminatingConstraint: CONTRACT_IMMUTABLE_FIELDS compile-time constant at ORDER 1, sum validation at ORDER 2, OCC write at ORDER 3. Any immutable field write attempt = BUILD_FAILURE.

teachingPoint: Escrow release requires a delivery gate before any funds move. Delivery must be validated before release to prevent a client from releasing funds without an accepted deliverable. LIFO compensation at milestone granularity ensures that on failure, the most recent milestone operation is reversed first — preventing partial escrow states. A timer gate-opener can auto-release after a configurable FREEDOM timeout when no dispute is raised.
positiveExample: T615 release path: validate delivery gate at ORDER 1 → release milestone funds at ORDER 2 → storeDocument(audit) at ORDER 3 → emit MilestoneReleased at ORDER 4. Dispute path: updateDocument(status:DISPUTED) → audit → emit MilestoneDisputed. LIFO compensation: [REFUND_MILESTONE, RESTORE_GIG_STATUS] run LIFO on failure.
negativeExample: T615 releases funds before validating delivery — client clicks release without uploading deliverable, funds escape escrow. Or T615 uses FIFO compensation — RESTORE_GIG_STATUS runs before REFUND_MILESTONE, leaving gig restored but funds still released.
discriminatingConstraint: Delivery gate validation BEFORE funds release. LIFO compensation at milestone granularity. Any FIFO compensation or release-before-delivery = BUILD_FAILURE.

teachingPoint: Reviews in a freelancer marketplace are single-direction: a client can review a freelancer and a freelancer can review a client, but neither party can submit two reviews in the same direction. Duplicate review detection must check direction before any write. The audit is append-only — once a review is submitted, it cannot be modified. Comments are excluded from PLATFORM_ONLY audit scope because they may contain PII.
positiveExample: T616: direction check (CLIENT_TO_FREELANCER or FREELANCER_TO_CLIENT) at ORDER 1 → duplicate check (searchDocuments for existing review in same direction) at ORDER 2 → write review at ORDER 3 → storeDocument(audit, {excludeComments: true}) at ORDER 4 → enqueue(FreelancerReviewSubmitted) at ORDER 5.
negativeExample: T616 allows a client to submit two reviews for the same freelancer in the same direction — reputation inflation attack. Or T616 includes comment content in PLATFORM_ONLY audit — PII leak via audit export. Or T616 allows review update after submission — reputation manipulation.
discriminatingConstraint: Direction check at ORDER 1, duplicate check at ORDER 2, append-only audit with comment excluded from PLATFORM_ONLY. Any duplicate direction allowed or comment in audit = BUILD_FAILURE.

teachingPoint: LIFO compensation at milestone granularity is the correct pattern for escrow failures in a freelancer marketplace. Each milestone is an independent escrow unit — compensation must reverse only the failed milestone, not all preceding milestones. LIFO ordering ensures the most recently started operation (escrow release) is reversed before restoring the gig status, preventing a window where gig is available again but funds are still in transit.
positiveExample: T615 compensation chain registered as [REFUND_MILESTONE, RESTORE_GIG_STATUS] forward-order. LIFO executor runs RESTORE_GIG_STATUS → REFUND_MILESTONE (reversed). Gig status restored only after refund confirmed — no race window.
negativeExample: T615 uses FIFO compensation — REFUND_MILESTONE runs first, then RESTORE_GIG_STATUS. If RESTORE_GIG_STATUS fails, gig is stuck in released state with refunded funds — inconsistent. Or T615 compensates all milestones when one fails — cascades irreversible refunds to milestones that completed successfully.
discriminatingConstraint: LIFO at milestone granularity — [REFUND_MILESTONE, RESTORE_GIG_STATUS] registered forward, executed reversed. FIFO or all-milestone cascade = BUILD_FAILURE.

teachingPoint: Reputation audit in a freelancer marketplace must be append-only — once a review is written, no update or delete is permitted. The audit record captures the review metadata but excludes free-text comment content because comments may contain PII that must not appear in PLATFORM_ONLY audit exports. The append-only constraint makes reputation signal provenance verifiable and tamper-evident.
positiveExample: T616: storeDocument(auditRecord, {excludeField: 'comment', knowledgeScope: 'PRIVATE'}) at ORDER 4 — no updateDocument on review records ever. Audit captures: reviewId, direction, rating, submittedAt, reviewerId, targetId. Comment excluded.
negativeExample: T616 includes comment in audit record with PLATFORM_ONLY scope — comment text appears in operator audit exports, leaking user PII. Or T616 allows updateDocument on review records — reviewers can retract negative reviews after being paid, corrupting reputation signal.
discriminatingConstraint: Append-only audit, comment excluded from PLATFORM_ONLY scope. Any updateDocument on review or comment in platform audit = BUILD_FAILURE.

keywords: BOLA ORDER 1 SETNX gig-accept-lock OCC bid status GigAccepted proposal acceptance lock idempotency freelancer
teachingPoint: Proposal acceptance requires three sequential guards: BOLA (ownership), SETNX (exclusivity), OCC (freshness). Missing any one guard creates a different class of bug: missing BOLA enables cross-tenant hijacking, missing SETNX enables double-acceptance, missing OCC enables stale-bid acceptance after withdrawal.
ironRules: BOLA validateOwnership(gigPosting.clientTenantId === ALS.tenantId) at ORDER 1 — before any lock or write
SETNX('gig-accept-lock:{gigId}') at ORDER 2 — exclusive acceptance lock
OCC bid status check at ORDER 3 — validate bid has not been withdrawn since lock acquired
storeDocument(audit) at ORDER 4 BEFORE enqueue(GigAccepted) — DNA-8
GigAccepted emitted at ORDER 5 only after all guards pass

keywords: CONTRACT_IMMUTABLE_FIELDS sum validation OCC milestone contract immutability gate FreelancerContractActivated T614
teachingPoint: Milestone contract management requires immutability guard at ORDER 1 (compile-time constant), sum validation at ORDER 2 (milestones sum === contractTotal), and OCC write at ORDER 3. Immutable fields (clientId, contractTotal, gigId) must never be updatable after contract activation.
ironRules: CONTRACT_IMMUTABLE_FIELDS compile-time constant checked at ORDER 1 — no storage for immutable field writes
Sum validation: milestones.reduce(sum, 0) === contractTotal at ORDER 2
storeDocumentWithOCC at ORDER 3 — not plain storeDocument
storeDocument(audit) at ORDER 4 BEFORE enqueue(FreelancerContractActivated) — DNA-8

keywords: delivery gate before release milestone escrow LIFO REFUND_MILESTONE RESTORE_GIG_STATUS MilestoneReleased MilestoneDisputed timer-gate-opener T615
teachingPoint: Escrow release must always validate delivery first. LIFO compensation at milestone granularity is the safe failure mode — RESTORE_GIG_STATUS runs before REFUND_MILESTONE in reverse order to prevent the window where the gig is available but funds are mid-transit.
ironRules: Delivery gate validation BEFORE any funds movement
LIFO compensation: [REFUND_MILESTONE, RESTORE_GIG_STATUS] registered forward, executed reversed
Dispute path: updateDocument(status:DISPUTED) only — never deleteDocument
Timer gate-opener from FREEDOM config — milestone_auto_release_days

keywords: single-direction review CLIENT_TO_FREELANCER FREELANCER_TO_CLIENT duplicate check append-only comment excluded PLATFORM_ONLY FreelancerReviewSubmitted T616
teachingPoint: Reviews are directional: CLIENT_TO_FREELANCER or FREELANCER_TO_CLIENT. Each direction can only be used once per engagement. Comment text must not appear in PLATFORM_ONLY audit exports to prevent PII leakage. The review record itself is append-only.
ironRules: Direction check (CLIENT_TO_FREELANCER or FREELANCER_TO_CLIENT) at ORDER 1
Duplicate direction check: searchDocuments for existing review in same direction at ORDER 2
Write review at ORDER 3 — storeDocument with knowledgeScope:PRIVATE
storeDocument(audit, exclude comment) at ORDER 4 BEFORE enqueue — DNA-8
FreelancerReviewSubmitted emitted at ORDER 5 — no updateDocument on reviews ever
